build.message: Build succeeds but has bundle size warnings (expected, non-blocking)

modalPerSection.message: Each of 6 sections has exactly 1 modal instance

closeButtonNotAbsolute.message: Close button is in flex justify-end wrapper (PHP line 120), not absolutely positioned

closeButtonAccessibility.message: Close button has aria-label, modal-close-btn class, and is focusable

scrollability.message: Modal content has overflow-y-auto and max-h-full classes (PHP line 119)

jsImplementation.message: JS uses optional chaining, event delegation, and module-level currentModal variable

issues.description: TypeScript compilation error: PostSelectorSortable onChange callback returns 'number | number[]' but selectedPosts attribute expects 'number[]'
issues.fix: Cast the value to number[] or handle both types: onChange={ ( value ) => setAttributes( { selectedPosts: Array.isArray( value ) ? value : [ value ] } ) }
issues.reason: TypeScript compilation fails, preventing type safety and potentially breaking builds in strict mode

passedChecks: Build compiles successfully (with expected bundle size warnings)
Each section has its own modal (6 sections × 1 modal each = 6 modals total)
Modal is INSIDE the section element (verified in PHP template line 135)
Close button is NOT absolutely positioned - uses flex justify-end wrapper (PHP line 120)
JS correctly finds modal within section using closest() and querySelector()
Close button has aria-label="Close video" (PHP line 123)
Close button has modal-close-btn class (PHP line 122)
Modal content has overflow-y-auto and max-h-full for scrollability (PHP line 119)
JS uses optional chaining (?.) for safe property access
JS uses event delegation for close button clicks

changes: Video modal moved inside <section> element -- each block instance has its own modal (static guard removed)
Close button is now in normal document flow, right-aligned via flex justify-end wrapper above the video container (no longer absolutely positioned)
Modal inner wrapper has overflow-y-auto + max-h-full + py-8 for scrollability on short viewports
JS reworked from single global modal to per-section approach: btn.closest('section.testimonials-carousel').querySelector('.testimonial-video-modal'), module-level currentModal variable
Scale transition selector changed from .relative to .testimonial-modal-content
focusAreas: Each section has its own modal
Modal is INSIDE the section element
Close button is NOT absolutely positioned
JS correctly finds modal within the section
